hero.question: Will Houston Astros win the 2026 American League Championship Series?
hero.prob_take: At 6.5%, the market is pricing the Astros' ALCS run as a near-lottery ticket, basically writing off their chances despite Houston's track record of deep playoff runs and a payroll built to compete right now. The Astros made the ALCS in 2024 and their core is intact, so you're getting paid pennies for what should realistically be a 10-12% probability in a wide-open AL.

daily_take.headline: Astros ALCS odds crushed 750 bps in 24 hours. Smart money knows something.
daily_take.deck: Houston's 2026 pennant chances just got nuked from 14% to 6.5% on $683K volume, a stunning reversal nobody saw coming. This isn't random noise. When a market moves that hard that fast on a question 18 months out, the crowd either discovered new information or repriced a fundamental mismatch. The play: dig into what triggered the selloff. Injury news, front office moves, or just market correction after being overbought? If it's the latter, 6.5% undervalues a team with current payroll flexibility. If it's real, you're watching the market work. Watch the 72-hour volume trend.
